faq.a1: You can convert between WebP, PNG, JPG (JPEG), AVIF, and ICO formats. AVIF is available when your browser supports it (Chrome 121+, Firefox 113+). All conversions happen in your browser using the Canvas API.
faq.q2: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.a2: No. All image processing happens locally in your browser. Your images never leave your device.
faq.q3: Can I convert multiple images at once?
faq.a3: Yes, you can drag and drop multiple images and they will all be converted to your selected format.
faq.q4: Can I use this tool offline?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page has loaded, the tool works completely offline. For the best experience, install PrivaDeck as a PWA from your browser.
faq.a5: There are no server-imposed limits. The maximum file size depends on your device's available memory and browser capabilities. Most modern devices handle files up to several hundred MB without issues.

intro.title: What is Image Format Converter?
intro.content:
Image Format Converter transforms images between popular formats including WebP, PNG, JPG, AVIF, and ICO. Need to convert a WebP image to PNG for compatibility, compress to AVIF for smaller files, or create an ICO favicon? This tool handles it instantly in your browser.

Supports batch conversion — drop multiple images and convert them all at once.

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Web Optimization: Convert images to WebP or AVIF for smaller file sizes and faster websites
  • Compatibility: Convert WebP or AVIF images to PNG or JPG for apps that don't support modern formats
  • Favicon Creation: Generate ICO favicons from PNG or JPG images
  • Social Media: Convert images to formats required by different platforms
  • Print Preparation: Convert web images to print-compatible formats

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Conversion
privacy.content:
Images are converted entirely in your browser using the HTML5 Canvas API. No images are uploaded to any server.

  • Your images never leave your device
  • No server processing, no cloud storage, no third-party access
  • Works offline after the page loads

faq.a1: Choose a compression preset (High Quality, Balanced, or Small File) or customize settings manually. The tool uses lossy compression to reduce file size while preserving visual quality. You can also convert between formats — AVIF and WebP typically offer 25–50% smaller files than JPEG at similar quality.
faq.a2: You can compress JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and BMP images. The tool also lets you convert between JPEG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF output formats, so you can compress and convert in one step. AVIF is available when your browser supports it.
faq.q3: Will my files be uploaded to a server?
faq.a3: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your data never leaves your device — nothing is uploaded to any server.
faq.q4: What do the compression presets do?
faq.a4: High Quality preserves maximum detail (90% quality, up to 10 MB). Balanced offers a good tradeoff (75% quality, up to 2 MB). Small File aggressively compresses (50% quality, up to 1 MB, max 1920px). Custom lets you fine-tune quality, file size, resolution, and dimensions manually.
faq.q5: Can I preserve or remove EXIF metadata?
faq.a5: Yes. The Preserve EXIF toggle lets you keep photo metadata (camera info, GPS, date) or strip it to reduce file size and protect privacy. EXIF is stripped by default to reduce file size and protect privacy.

Ideal for optimizing website images, reducing email attachment sizes, preparing social media uploads, or saving device storage.

exif-editor.name: EXIF Editor
exif-editor.description: View and edit photo metadata — GPS, camera, dates, copyright.
exif-editor.metaTitle: EXIF Editor - View & Edit Photo Metadata Online | PrivaDeck
exif-editor.metaDescription: Free online EXIF editor. View and edit GPS, camera, dates, copyright, and IPTC fields in your photos. Works in your browser — no upload. Supports JPEG, PNG, WebP.
exif-editor.keywords: exif editor, edit exif online, view exif, photo metadata editor, gps coordinates editor, edit camera info, remove gps, iptc editor

f1.title: Complete Metadata Inspection
f1.description: Read every EXIF, IPTC, and XMP field from JPEG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, AVIF, and TIFF. Fields are grouped by camera, lens, date, GPS, and copyright so you can scan a photo's full digital history at a glance.

f2.title: Edit Key Fields In Place
f2.description: Change date taken, GPS coordinates, camera and lens model, author, copyright, title, and description in one form. No format conversion, no pixel re-encoding — your original photo is preserved.

f3.title: One-Click Privacy Clear
f3.description: Dedicated buttons wipe the GPS coordinate or shooting date without touching the rest of the metadata. Perfect for stripping location before posting to social media or sharing with clients.

f4.title: Preserve Original Format
f4.description: Writes edits back into JPEG, PNG, and WebP in the original container — no re-encoding, no quality loss, no surprise format switch. Your PNG stays a PNG and your WebP stays a WebP.

f5.title: JSON / CSV Export
f5.description: Dump the full metadata record — including maker notes and rare tags — as JSON or spreadsheet-ready CSV. Ideal for audits, forensic work, legal documentation, or feeding into other pipelines.

f6.title: 100% Local, Zero Upload
f6.description: Every read and every write happens in your browser. Photos never leave your device, the GPS preview is rendered locally, and the whole tool keeps working offline once the page has loaded.

viewer.readOnlyNotice: {format} files can be viewed but not edited. This tool preserves your original format — it does not convert between formats.

actions.openInMapTooltip: Opens an external map service. Coordinates are sent to that provider.

messages.noExifFound: No EXIF data found in this file.

faq.a1: You can edit the most common fields: date taken, GPS coordinates (latitude/longitude), camera model, lens model, author, copyright, title, and description. Rare or device-specific fields are shown as read-only so you don't accidentally corrupt them.
faq.a2: No. All reading and writing happens locally in your browser. Photos never leave your device.
faq.q3: Why can't I edit HEIC, AVIF, or TIFF files?
faq.a3: This tool preserves your original file format instead of converting to JPEG. In-place editing for HEIC, AVIF, and TIFF requires complex container rewriting and is not supported in this version. You can still view all metadata in these formats.
faq.q4: How do I completely remove the GPS location?
faq.a4: Click the Clear GPS button in the GPS section of the edit form. All other metadata (camera, date, copyright) is preserved in the saved file.
faq.a5: A complete dump of every EXIF, IPTC, and XMP field the tool could parse from the file. Includes camera details, GPS, timestamps, maker notes, and more. Useful for audits, forensic work, or feeding into other tools.

intro.title: What is EXIF Editor?
intro.content:
EXIF Editor lets you inspect and edit the hidden metadata stored inside a photo — GPS location, camera model, lens, shooting date, author, copyright, and more. Unlike most online editors, it keeps your original file format intact and does all processing in your browser.

Perfect for correcting a wrong timestamp after travel, stripping GPS before sharing, or stamping copyright onto an exported photo.

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Before Sharing: Strip the GPS coordinate from a photo before posting to social media
  • After Travel: Fix a wrong date or time zone on a photo taken while the camera clock was off
  • Copyright Stamping: Add author, copyright, and caption to an exported photo
  • Forensics & Audit: Export the complete EXIF/IPTC/XMP to JSON or CSV for documentation
  • Privacy Review: Inspect exactly what metadata a photo carries before deciding what to remove

privacy.title: Privacy-First EXIF Editing
privacy.content:
Uploading a photo to a server to edit its privacy-revealing metadata would defeat the purpose. Everything happens in your browser.

  • Your photos never leave your device
  • The GPS preview is drawn locally — no map tile requests that would leak the location
  • Opening a location on OpenStreetMap is strictly user-initiated via the external link button
  • Works offline once the page has loaded

f1.title: Pick exactly what to remove
f1.description: Eight categories—GPS, camera & lens, shooting settings, date & time, author, description, thumbnail, XMP—each with its own checkbox. Keep what you want, strip the rest.

f2.title: GPS location gone in one click
f2.description: Uncheck everything else, keep only GPS, and your photo loses its geotag instantly—share safely without leaking where it was shot.

f3.title: Binary stripping, no re-encoding
f3.description: When you remove everything, we strip segments directly at the byte level—no JPEG recompression, no PNG re-export. The output pixels are identical to the input.

f4.title: JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF
f4.description: Works on every common photo format. Selective removal on JPEG / PNG / WebP, full strip on AVIF and HEIC.

f5.title: 100% in your browser
f5.description: Nothing is uploaded. Every byte stays on your device. Once the page is loaded, the tool works completely offline.

f6.title: ICC color profile preserved
f6.description: We never touch your color profile—wide-gamut JPEGs and print-ready PNGs keep their ICC_PROFILE segment intact.

remove-exif.categoryHint: All categories are selected by default. Uncheck any category to keep that data.

remove-exif.noSelection: Select at least one category to remove.

intro.content:
Remove EXIF Data strips all metadata from your photos including GPS location, camera settings, timestamps, and device information. Protect your privacy before sharing photos online by removing data that reveals where and when a photo was taken.

Essential for privacy-conscious users sharing photos on social media, forums, or websites.

faq.q1: What's the difference between flipping and rotating?
faq.a1: Flipping mirrors the image (left/right or top/bottom). Rotating turns it 90 degrees clockwise or counter-clockwise. You can combine all four — for example, flip horizontally and then rotate 90 degrees clockwise.
faq.a2: No. The image is redrawn at its original resolution. JPEG images are re-encoded at 92% quality.
faq.q3: Will my files be uploaded to a server?
faq.a3: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your data never leaves your device — nothing is uploaded to any server.
faq.q4: Can I use this tool offline?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page has loaded, the tool works completely offline. For the best experience, install PrivaDeck as a PWA from your browser.
faq.a5: There are no server-imposed limits. The maximum file size depends on your device's available memory and browser capabilities. Most modern devices handle files up to several hundred MB without issues.
faq.q6: Can transforms be stacked or undone?
faq.a6: Yes. Operations stack — clicking rotate then flip applies both. Click Reset to return to the original image. Transforms are only baked into the final file when you click Download.

intro.title: What is Flip & Rotate?
intro.content:
Flip & Rotate transforms images in your browser with four operations: horizontal flip, vertical flip, 90 degree clockwise rotation, and 90 degree counter-clockwise rotation. Operations stack and the preview updates instantly as you click.

Useful for correcting mirrored selfies, rotating sideways phone photos, fixing scan orientation, or preparing images for design work.

heic-convert.name: HEIC Converter
heic-convert.description: Convert a single HEIC/HEIF photo to JPG or PNG with Apple-correct orientation, plus manual rotate and flip.
heic-convert.metaTitle: HEIC to JPG/PNG Converter with Rotate & Flip - Free Online
heic-convert.metaDescription: Convert one HEIC or HEIF photo to JPG or PNG with automatic Apple-style orientation correction and manual rotate & flip. 100% local, no upload.
heic-convert.keywords: heic to jpg, heic to png, heif converter, iphone photo converter, heic orientation, heic rotate, heic flip, apple photo converter

f1.title: Apple-Style Auto Orientation
f1.description: Reads the EXIF Orientation tag (values 1–8) and bakes it into the pixels, matching what you see in the iPhone Photos app — no more sideways photos on Windows.

f2.title: Manual Rotate & Flip
f2.description: Fine-tune in 90° steps and mirror horizontally or vertically on top of the automatic correction. Every adjustment redraws the live preview instantly.

f3.title: Burst Photos → Animated GIF
f3.description: Detects iPhone burst HEIC files automatically and exports them as a single animated GIF with a live encoding progress indicator. Orientation is applied to every frame.

f4.title: JPG, PNG and GIF Output
f4.description: Decode once, then switch freely between JPEG, PNG, or GIF (for bursts) — no need to re-upload the original HEIC file.

f5.title: Adjustable JPG Quality
f5.description: Tune JPEG output quality from 10% to 100% with a simple slider to balance file size against visual fidelity.

f6.title: 100% Local — No Upload
f6.description: Decoding (heic2any), EXIF parsing (exifr) and re-encoding all run in your browser. Your photo never leaves your device and the tool works offline as a PWA.

heic-convert.decodingFailed: Could not decode this HEIC file.
heic-convert.appleNote: EXIF orientation was detected and automatically applied, matching how Apple Photos displays this image.
heic-convert.burstDetected: Burst photo detected ({count} frames) — animated GIF available

faq.a1: HEIC (High Efficiency Image Container) is the default image format used by Apple devices since iOS 11. It offers roughly half the file size of JPEG at similar quality, but is not natively readable on Windows or older Android devices.
faq.q2: Is my image uploaded to a server?
faq.a2: No. Decoding, EXIF parsing, rotate/flip rendering, and re-encoding all happen locally in your browser. Your photo never leaves your device.
faq.q3: Will my files be uploaded to a server?
faq.a3: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your data never leaves your device — nothing is uploaded to any server.
faq.q4: Can I use this tool offline?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page has loaded, the tool works completely offline. For the best experience, install PrivaDeck as a PWA from your browser.
faq.a5: There are no server-imposed limits. The maximum file size depends on your device's available memory and browser capabilities. Most modern devices handle files up to several hundred MB without issues.
faq.q6: Why do my iPhone photos appear sideways on Windows?
faq.a6: iPhones save pixels in sensor orientation and record the intended rotation in the EXIF Orientation tag. Apple Photos and most modern viewers honor that tag, but some Windows apps and older viewers ignore it, so the photo looks rotated. This tool bakes the orientation into the pixels during conversion, so the downloaded JPG or PNG looks correct everywhere.
faq.q7: The orientation is still wrong after conversion. What can I do?
faq.a7: Use the Rotate 90° left/right and Flip Horizontal/Vertical controls above the preview. They stack on top of the automatic EXIF correction, so you can dial in any orientation before downloading.
faq.q8: Is EXIF metadata kept in the output?
faq.a8: The orientation tag is effectively applied to the pixels, so the output no longer needs it. Other EXIF data such as GPS location, camera model and capture date are not carried over — the converter emits fresh JPG or PNG pixel data only. If you want to guarantee a metadata-free file, pair this with the Remove EXIF tool.

intro.title: What is HEIC Converter?
intro.content:
HEIC Converter turns a single Apple HEIC or HEIF photo into a widely compatible JPG or PNG file, entirely inside your browser. It is tuned for the way iPhones actually store photos: the tool reads the EXIF Orientation tag, applies it to the pixels, and matches what you see in the Apple Photos app on your device.

If the photo comes from an Apple device, the tool also detects the make and model and shows a small badge so you know the orientation has been corrected automatically.

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Sharing iPhone photos with Windows or Android users: convert to JPG without the sideways-photo problem.
  • Posting to social media that does not accept HEIC, while keeping the photo oriented the way you shot it.
  • Sending photos over email with guaranteed portrait or landscape orientation.
  • Importing into Photoshop, Lightroom or design tools that mishandle HEIC orientation.
  • Fixing sideways or upside-down photos with manual rotate and flip before downloading.
  • Printing services that require JPG or PNG with correct orientation.

privacy.title: Privacy-First HEIC Conversion
privacy.content:
Every step runs locally in your browser.

  • HEIC decoding uses the heic2any library, running as WebAssembly on your machine.
  • EXIF metadata is read with exifr, also client-side.
  • Your photo and its metadata never leave your device — nothing is uploaded.
  • Works offline after the page loads; safe for personal and sensitive photos.
